Acquisitions & M&A.pdf_text: To, BSE Limited, Floor 25, P J Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai – 400001 Script Code: 543963 Ref: Intimation with regards to “Shelter Pharma” secured order from First Vet Veterinary Medicines Trading LLC, UAE pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligation and Disclosure Requirements) Regulation, 2015 Pursuant to regulation 30(4) of the SEBI (Listing Obligation and Disclosure Requirements) Regulation, 2015, we are pleased to inform you that “Shelter Pharma Limited” secured a noteworthy order from First Vet Veterinary Medicines Trading LLC, UAE for Supply of veterinary / animal healthcare products total order worth 18,300 $. The details of the order as per SEBI is been annexed herewith this letter. Kindly take the note of above information on record and oblige. About Shelter Pharma Limited: Shelter Pharma Limited is a leading pharmaceutical and nutraceutical company known for its unwavering dedication to research, innovation, and quality. Founded with a vision to provide accessible and effective healthcare solutions, we have consistently delivered products that adhere to the highest industry standards. Our state-of-the-art facilities, world-class team of experts, and commitment to ethical practices have made us a trusted name in the industry. At Shelter Pharma Limited, our mission is to promote well-being and health through innovative, high-quality nutraceuticals. We take immense pride in being recognized for our commitment to producing safe and effective products that enhance the quality of life for individuals across the globe. Our passion for innovation drives us to develop nutraceutical products that empower individuals to lead healthier lives. We are guided by a strong code of ethics and a deep sense of responsibility to society. For any inquiries or additional information, please feel free to contact our Investor Relations team. Kindly take the above on record and oblige. Thanking You. FOR, SHELTER PHARMA LIMITED ___________________________________ MUSTAQIM N

Kindly take the same on record. Thanking you, Sincerely yours, For Bharti Airtel Limited Rohit Krishan Puri Company Secretary & Compliance Officer Bharti Airtel Limited (a Bharti Enterprise) Regd. Office: Airtel Center, Plot No. 16, Udyog Vihar, Phase-IV, Gurugram - 122015, India Corporate Office: Bharti Crescent, 1, Nelson Mandela Road, Vasant Kunj, Phase II, New Delhi - 110070, India T.: +91-124-4222222, F.: +91-124-4248063, Email: compliance.officer@bharti.in, Website: www.airtel.in CIN: L74899HR1995PLC095967Customer Day 2026: Airtel Reinforces its Culture of Customer Obsession A renewed focus on raising its delivered experience and solving for customer problems at scale New Delhi, 12 March 2026: Airtel today marked Customer Day 2026, a mass movement within the organization focused on understanding customer pain points and continuously improving its delivered experiences. Nearly twenty thousand Airtel employees—from leadership teams to functional specialists—stepped out of their offices and into the field, working alongside frontline teams to engage directly with customers, shadowing field engineers, visiting homes and shops, and interacting at retail and service centres to gain an unfiltered view of the issues that matter most. Customer Day at Airtel serves as a deliberate pause for the organisation to introspect and ask a simple but critical question: what more can we do for our customers? The PPAs has been signed for a tenor of 25 years on 2 March 2026. Credit Ratings.pdf_text: To Date: 12.03.2026 BSE Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Dalal Street, Mumbai-400001 Maharashtra, India (Scrip Code: 544151) Sub: Chatha Foods Operational Update Amid Recent LPG Supply Concerns Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we are enclosing herewith a General Announcement issued by the Company regarding the potential LPG supply constraints in India arising from global geopolitical developments. The detailed communication in this regard is enclosed as Annexure A. This communication is being shared for the information of the stakeholders of the Company. We request you to kindly take the announcement on record. The above information is also being available on the Company’s website at https://cfpl.net.in/. Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For Chatha Foods Limited Priyanka Oberoi Company Secretary & Compliance Officer(Annexure A) General Announcement: Chatha Foods Operational Update Amid Recent LPG Supply Concerns In light of the ongoing discussions around potential LPG supply constraints in India arising from global geopolitical developments, Chatha Foods Limited would like to inform its stakeholders regarding the Company’s manufacturing operations. The Company’s production processes are not dependent on LPG as a primary energy source. Our facilities are designed to operate through electricity and diesel-powered systems, enabling continuity of operations independent of LPG availability. Accordingly, any potential disruption or shortage in LPG supply is not expected to have a material impact on the Company’s manufacturing activities at this time. Chatha Foods continues to operate normally and remains focused on maintaining consistent production and supply to its partners and customers. The Company will continue to monitor the broader energy supply environment and take appropriate steps, where necessary, to ensure operational stability. ****

Management Changes.pdf_text: Dated: 12.03.2026 To Department of Corporate Services, BSE Limited, 25th Floor, P.J. Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai-400001 Sub: Disclosure pursuant to Regulation 30 of LODR Regulations, 2015 Sir/Madam, Pursuant to the Regulation 30 of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we would like to inform you that the company has partnered with Parsavnath Advisors, Delhi, to offer its Early-Wage-Access program to the employees of the firm. This partnership aims to provide an instant financial relief to the employees by allowing them to access part of their salaries seamlessly throughout the month. The Company, in line with its vision to expand its offerings and to serve retail customers at large had been working on a salary advance solution. This product entails tying up with employers to offer short term loans as salary advance. The amount lent is then collected through salary deduction. This is for your information and records. Management Changes.headline: Reply to the clarification sought with reference to the movement in volume
Management Changes.pdf_text: Date: March 12, 2026 To BSE Surveillance BSE Ltd P J Towers Dalal Street Mumbai - 400 001 Scrip Code: 544485 Dear Sir/Madam, Sub: Reply to the Clarification sought on movement in price of the shares across Exchanges of Shares of the Company-Regulation 30 of the SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015. Ref.: Your Email reference No. L/SURV/ONL/PV/SG/ 2025-2026 / 971 dated March 12, 2026 With reference to your above-mentioned email in respect of clarification sought on price movement of our scrip, kindly note that in compliance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, we hereby inform you that there is no pending information or announcement from the company which is/are price sensitive that could have led to significant movement in the price behaviour of the Company's shares. Further, as on date our Company does not have any undisclosed/potentially price sensitive information/event /announcement, that is required to be disclosed to the stock exchange under Regulation 30 of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, which in our opinion, may have a bearing on the price/volume of our Company's security. Therefore, we believe that the recent movement in price of our equity shares is purely due to market driven conditions. Further, we would like to assure you that Company will ensure stock exchange(s) are duly informed of all the information/ announcements required to be disclosed under applicable regulations, as and when required. We hope the above clarifies and request you to kindly take the same on your records. Thanking you, For Regaal Resources Limited Tinku Kumar Gupta Company Secretary and Compliance Officer

Yours Faithfully, For, GOPAL SNACKS LIMITED CS Mayur Gangani Head — Legal & Compliance cum Company Secretary Membership No. F9980 Encls: a/aBusiness Updates Rajkot, 12th March 2026: Gopal Snacks Limited, (“Gopal Snacks Ltd” or the “Company”) (BSE: 544140; NSE: GOPAL) a leading FMCG company in India renowned for its high- quality snack products, has announced a business update regarding the ongoing gas supply shortage. Recently Government of India has issued Notification on restriction of usage of Gas for Commercial use due to tensions in Middle East, Prioritizing the same for Domestic use. This is to clarify that, Company is already using Bio coal at its Modasa and Nagpur Facilities and its manufacturing facilities are running smoothly and will be able to serve its customers demand without any interruption. Gopal Snacks has always been looking to focus on taking strategic steps to use alternative sources of fuel, making it more cost effective and reducing the dependency on single source of fuel to overcome any such unforeseen challenges. Management Changes.headline: Intimation under SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 - Co-Lending Partnership with M/s. IIFL Finance Limited.
Management Changes.pdf_text: केंद्रीय कायाालय INVESTORS RELATION DIVISION Central Office CO:IRD:2025:26:269 Date: 12.03.2026 National Stock Exchange of India Limited BSE Limited Exchange Plaza, Corporate Relationship Dept., Plot No. C/1, ‘G’ Block, Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Bandra-Kurla Complex, Dalal Street, Fort, Bandra (E), Mumbai-400 051 Mumbai-400001 Scrip code – CENTRALBK Scrip Code – 532885 Dear Sir/Madam, Sub: Intimation under SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 – Co-Lending Partnership with M/s. IIFL Finance Limited. We would like to inform that Central Bank of India has entered into Co-Lending Partnership with M/s. IIFL Finance Limited to offer Loans at competitive rates, subject to compliance with the applicable law(s) including the revised Co-Lending Arrangements (CLA) guidelines dated 28.11.2025 issued by Reserve Bank of India (RBI). The participation by both the entities in this Co-Lending arrangement will result in greater expansion of portfolio by Central Bank of India and M/s. IIFL Finance Limited. M/s. IIFL Finance Limited is a retail-focused non-banking financial company (NBFC) registered with the Reserve Bank of India. The NBFC is a leading player in the gold loan business across India. The Mumbai headquartered IIFL Finance Ltd. has a wide network of 4,761 branches and a wide customer base, mostly from the underserved segments. As on December 31, 2025, IIFL Finance Limited reported assets under management (AUM) of Rs.49,027.00 crores. Under the arrangement, M/s. IIFL Finance Limited will originate loan proposals, which will be processed by both lenders as per jointly formulated credit parameters and eligibility criteria. The borrower will benefit from the blended interest rate charged. M/s. IIFL Finance Limited will service the loan account throughout the life cycle of the loan. The Co-Lending arrangement is expected to help both the entities and the end borrower and will also help to expand customer outreach across India. Please take th

Management Changes.pdf_text: 12th March 2026 BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze JeeJeeBhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, Dalal Street, 5th Floor, Plot No. C-1, G Block, Mumbai - 400 001 Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra (E) Mumbai - 400 051 SCRIP CODE: 523367 SCRIP CODE: DCMSHRIRAM Sub : Outcome of Board Meeting dated 12th March 2026 Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (‘Listing Regulations’), we hereby inform you that the Board of Directors of the Company at its meeting held today i.e., 12th March 2026 has approved the following: - Equity Investment of upto Rs.87 crores, in one or more tranches, for minimum 26% equity stake in one or more Special Purpose Vehicles, to be created for setting up a Renewable Energy Power project for its Bharuch Plant; and - proposal for incurring capital expenditure of upto to Rs. 130 crores, towards related infrastructure development. This will enable the Company to obtain ~ 48 MW of additional Renewable Power supply (~ 30 MW round the clock at 75% Capacity Utilization Factor) for its Bharuch Plant, thereby taking the total RE power provision for the Bharuch Plant from the present peak of 50.4MW to 98.4MW. The indicative time line for completion of the above project will be around June 2027. The details required under Regulation 30 of SEBI Listing Regulations read with SEBI Circular No. HO/49/14/14(7)2025-CFD-POD2/I/3762/2026 dated 30th January 2026, for the above investment proposal will be provided separately in due course, once details/other terms are finalised. The meeting of the Board of Directors of the Company commenced at 3.00 p.m. and concluded at 3.50 p.m. Kindly take the above information on record. Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For DCM Shriram Limited (Deepak Gupta) Company Secretary & Compliance Officer

AGM / EGM / Postal Ballot.pdf_text: Date: 12-03-2026 To, Corporate Relationship Department BSE Limited, Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai - 400 001 Sub: Covering Letter for Corrigendum I to the EGM Notice dated March 02, 2026 Scrip Code: 538579 | ISIN: INE641Q01019 Dear Sirs/Madam, This is with reference to the Notice of the Extraordinary General Meeting (EGM) of M/s Zinema Media and Entertainment Limited scheduled to be held on Saturday, March 28, 2026, at 03.30 P.M. through Video Conferencing (“VC”)/Other Audio Visual Means (“OAVM”). The Company is issuing the enclosed Corrigendum I to provide a necessary update to the original Notice. Specifically, please be advised that the name of Mr. C.R. Vasudevan has been removed from the list of proposed allottees under Item No. 1 regarding the preferential issue of equity shares. All other resolutions, including the issuance of sweat equity shares to Directors (Item No. 2), the acquisition of equity stake in M/s Beontyme Technologies Private Limited (Item No. 3), preferential issue of equity stake in M/s Tulsea Media Private Limited (Item No. 4), and the increase in authorized share capital (Item No. 5), remain unchanged and as stated in the original Notice.  Integration: This Corrigendum should be read as an integral part of the EGM Notice dated March 02, 2026.  E-Voting: The remote e-voting period remains from Wednesday, March 25, 2026 (09:00 A.M.) to Friday, March 27, 2026 (05:00 P.M.). The above documents are also available on the Company's website i.e. https://zinema.co.in, on the website of the Stock Exchanges i.e. BSE Limited (BSE) www.bseindia.com respectively, and the website of National Securities Depository Limited (NSDL) https://www.evoting.nsdl.com. You are requested to kindly take the above information on your records. ZINEMA MEDIA & ENTERTAINMENT LIMITED Registered Office – New Door Number 57, Old Door No.57, Sathya dev Avenue MRC Nagar, Raja Annamalaipuram, Raja Annamalaipuram, Chennai: 600028 CIN: L24241TN1984PLC096136 Email

Tel no.: +918779558264, Website: www.krishival.com, Email: cs@krishival.com March 12, 2026 To, National Stock Exchange of India Limited BSE Limited Exchange Plaza, Bandra Kurla Complex Bandra (E), Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai-400051 Mumbai - 400 001 NSE Symbol: KRISHIVAL BSE Scrip Code: 544416 Subject: Outcome of Board meeting Dear Sir / Madam, This is to inform you that, the Board of Directors of the Company at its meeting held on Thursday, March 12, 2026 inter alia, has considered and approved the following: 1. Grant of Loan to our subsidiary company, Melt 'N' Mellow Foods Private Limited (Ice Cream Division) subject to the approval of the Members of the Company. This is in suppression to the limit approved by the Members of the Company in their Extra- Ordinary General Meeting held on June 26, 2025. 2. Conversion of all loan into equity shares in one or more tranches at a future date, as may be determined, subject to the approval of the Members of the Company. 3. Approval of Related Party Transactions pursuant to Regulation 23(9) of SEBI (LODR), 2015, subject to the approval of the Members of the Company. 4. Approval of Postal Ballot Notice. 5. The e-voting period begins on Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 10.00 a.m. and ends on Monday, April 13, 2026 at 5.00 p.m. During this period, members of the Company, holding shares as on the cut- off date i.e. Friday, March 06, 2026 may cast their vote electronically. The e-voting module shall be disabled by CDSL for voting thereafter. 6. The voting rights of members shall be in proportion to their share of the paid-up equity share capital of the Company as on the Cut-off Date i.e. Friday, March 06, 2026. 7. The result of the e-voting shall be published by the Company Secretary of the Company on or before Tuesday, April 14, 2026. 8. The Company has appointed M/s.

Board Decisions.headline: Outcome Of Board Meeting
Board Decisions.pdf_text: Date: March 12, 2026 To, BSE Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalai Street, Mumbai-400 011 BSE SCRIP SYMBOL: KATIPATANG; BSE SCRIP CODE: 531126 Subject: Outcome of Board Meeting held on March 12, 2026 Dear Sir/ Ma’am, In accordance with Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“SEBI Listing Regulations”) and in reference to the outcome of Board meeting held on March 03, 2026, we would like to inform you that the Board of Directors of the Company at their meeting held today i.e., Thursday, 12th March, 2026 at the Corporate Office of the Company at 504, Savitri Cinema Complex, Greater Kailash–II, New Delhi-110 048, has inter-alia, considered and approved the following businesses: 1. Preferential Issue of upto 14,58,333 Equity Shares of face value of Rs. 10/- each at an issue price of Rs. 24/- each, being the price determined in accordance with SEBI ICDR Regulations, 2018, to be issued to shareholders of Agnetta International Private Limited towards acquisition of 100% stake in Agnetta International Private Limited through share swap arrangement for an aggregate investment value of Rs. 3,50,00,000/- (“Share Swap 1”). 2. Preferential Issue of upto 3,87,280 Equity Shares of face value of Rs. 10/- each at an issue price of Rs. 24/- each, being the price determined in accordance with SEBI ICDR Regulations, 2018, to be issued to shareholders of Empyrean Spirits Private Limited (ESPL) towards acquisition of remaining 2% stake (38780 equity shares) in ESPL through share swap arrangement for an aggregate investment value of Rs. 92,94,720/- (“Share Swap 2”). 3. Approved the Draft Notice of Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) of the Company to be held on Monday, April 06, 2026 & requisite authorization thereof. The Board meeting commenced at 01:00 pm (IST) & conclude at 02:15 pm (IST). Details as required under the Regulation 30 of SEBI Listing Regulations read with relevant circulars is attached as ‘Annexure A’ KATI PATANG LI

Warrant Conversions.headline: Press Release dated 12.03.2026
Warrant Conversions.pdf_text: PRESS RELEASE CAMIKARA BECOMES THE WORLD’S BEST INDIAN RUM. BEATS GLOBAL RUM BRANDS After Indian single malt whisky captured global attention, Indian rum is now stepping into the spotlight — with Camikara leading the charge th 12 March 2026, New Delhi - Over the past decade, Indian single malt whisky has transformed the country’s reputation in global spirits, challenging long-established whisky regions and earning international acclaim. Now, a similar shift may be unfolding in another category deeply rooted in India’s agricultural heritage — Rum. In a landmark moment for Indian spirits, Camikara — India’s first pure cane juice aged rum — has secured top honours at two of the world’s most respected rum competitions, signalling the emergence of India as a serious contender in premium rum. At the Global Rum & Cachaça Masters Awards 2026 in the United Kingdom, Camikara 8-Year- Old was awarded the prestigious ‘Master Medal’, the competition’s highest distinction. In the same competition, Camikara 3-Year-Old secured its second consecutive ‘Gold Medal’, making Camikarathe only Indian rum to win both Master and Gold in the same year. Recognition soon followed in the United States. At the Aged Rum category tasting conducted by The Fifty Best, Camikara 8YO received a ‘Double Gold’ Medal, a distinction reserved for spirits that receive unanimous top scores from all judges. In the International blend category, Camikara 3YO also secured ‘Gold’, reinforcing the brand’s consistency across both expressions and international tasting panels. Piccadily Agro Industries Ltd. Sohil Yusuf Dabhoya Whole Time Director DIN: 07112947For immediate release KPI Green Energy Limited energises 35 MWp to reach 589 MWp energised capacity out of 2.17 GWp IPP Portfolio KPI Green Energy Limited (“KPI Green” or “the Company”) has recently energised an additional 35 MWp of solar capacity, taking its total operational IPP capacity to 589 MWp. With projects currently in hand, the Company remains well on track to commission an additional 1,582 MWp in the near term, which will expand its total IPP portfolio to 2.17 GWp. This continued capacity addition further strengthens KPI Green’s position as a leading Independent Power Producer (IPP). Debt & Finance.pdf_text: March 12, 2026 BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited P J Towers, Exchange plaza, Dalal Street, Bandra-Kurla Complex, Mumbai – 400001 Bandra (E), Mumbai – 400051 Scrip Code: 539254 Scrip Code: ADANIENSOL Dear Sir, Sub: Intimation regarding Issuance and Redemption of Senior Secured Notes. This is to inform that Adani Transmission Step One Limited (“ATSOL”), a wholly owned subsidiary of Adani Energy Solutions Limited (“AESL”), has outstanding U.S.$500,000,000 Senior Secured Notes due July 2026 (“Existing Notes”). ATSOL Global IFSC Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of ATSOL incorporated in GIFT City, has executed a Note Purchase Agreement for the issuance of U.S.$500,000,000 Senior Secured Notes due 2041 (the “Proposed Notes”) to fully refinance ATSOL’s Existing notes. The Proposed Notes have been assigned Investment Grade ratings of BBB- /Stable by Fitch Ratings and Baa3/Stable by Moody’s Ratings. Further, ATSOL has issued a notice of redemption to the holders of the Existing Notes. You are requested to take the same on your records. MoUs & Agreements.pdf_text: A C C R E D I T E D IS O/IE C 17 0 2 1 CERTIFICATIONB ODY Date: March 12, 2026 To To BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited 25th Floor, PJ Towers Exchange Plaza , C-1, Block G Dalal Street, B a n d r a K u r l a C o mplex, Bandra (E) Mumbai-400001 M u m b a i – 4 0 0 0 5 1 Scrip Code: 524654 S y m b o l : N A T C A P SUQ Sub: Press Release Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, please find enclosed herewith the copy of press release in connection with Natural Biogenex Private Limited (“Material Subsidiary”) has entered into “Framework & Contract Manfacturing agreements” with Fermbox Bio Private Limited. We request you to kindly take the aforesaid information on record. Thanking You Yours Faithfully, For Natural Capsules Limited Pranjal Deshmukh Company Secretary & Compliance Officer M.No: A66119P RE S S & M E D I A RE L E A S E Natural Biogenex and Fermbox Bio Enter Strategic Framework and Manufacturing Agreements to Strengthen Fermentation-Based CDMO Capabilities Natural Biogenex Private Limited, a subsidiary of Natural Capsules Limited, has executed definitive Framework and Contract Manufacturing Agreements with Fermbox Bio Private Limited to jointly advance fermentation-based Contract Development and Manufacturing (CDMO) services. Under this collaboration, Fermbox will provide advanced fermentation technology and equipment placement of up to ₹60 crore at NBPL’s state-of-the-art facility in Tumkur, while NBPL will undertake licensed manufacturing, quality assurance and regulatory compliance. The partnership positions the Company to serve global biotechnology and biopharma clients with integrated, GMP-compliant manufacturing capacity and enhanced process efficiency. The global biotech and biopharma CDMO market continues to present strong growth opportunities, driven by rising demand, increasing outsourcing, and more complex biomanufacturin

Analyst / Investor Meets.pdf_text: Date: March 12, 2026 To, Listing Department Listing Department BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Bandra Kurla Complex Dalal Street, Fort Bandra East Mumbai-400 001 Mumbai – 400 051 BSE Scrip Code: 539289 NSE Symbol: AURUM Dear Sir/Madam, Sub.: Media Release - Aurum PropTech Exits non-core Assets for INR 112 Crores, Builds War Chest to Scale AI Led PropTech. Pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements), Regulations, 2015, please find enclosed a copy of the Media Release issued by the Company with regard to the captioned subject. The Board of Directors, at its meeting held today, i.e., March 12, 2026, has approved the sale of Buildings Q5 and Q6 at Millennium Business Park, Navi Mumbai. The Media Release will also be available on the Company’s website at www.aurumproptech.in. You are requested to take the above on record. Thanking you. For Aurum PropTech Limited Sonia Jain Company Secretary & Compliance OfficerMEDIA RELEASE Aurum PropTech Exits non-core Assets for INR 112 Crores, Builds War Chest to Scale AI led PropTech Mumbai, India – March 12, 2026: Aurum PropTech Limited today announced that its Board of Directors has approved the sale of Buildings Q5 and Q6 at Millennium Business Park, Navi Mumbai, for a total consideration of INR 112 Crores, approximately 15% above the valuation provided by the valuers. The transaction forms part of Aurum PropTech’s broader strategic shift toward AI-driven PropTech platforms, with the company committing significant capital toward building advanced artificial intelligence capabilities across the real estate ecosystem. The proceeds from the transaction will further strengthen Aurum’s growing AI investment pool and support the accelerated development of intelligent digital infrastructure for the real estate sector. The assets have a approximate book value of INR 27 Crores, and the transaction will generate a substantial profit, thereby enhancing the

Press Releases.headline: Zydus expands companion diagnostics portfolio with the launch of AI-powered Continuous Glucose Monitor DiasensTM and GlucoLiveTM.
Press Releases.pdf_text: March 12, 2026 BSE Limited Code: 532321 P J Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai-400001 National Stock Exchange of India Limited Code: Zyduslife Exchange Plaza, C/1, Block G, Bandra-Kurla Complex, Bandra (East), Mumbai-400051 Re.: Press Release Dear Sir / Madam, Please find enclosed a copy of press release dated March 12, 2026, titled “Zydus expands companion diagnostics portfolio with the launch of AI-powered Continuous Glucose Monitor DiasensTM and GlucoLiveTM”. The contents of the press release give full details. Please bring the aforesaid news to the notice of the members of the exchange and the investors’ at large. Yours faithfully, For, Zydus Lifesciences Limited Dhaval N. Soni Company Secretary and Compliance Officer Membership No. FCS7063 Encl.: As aboveZydus expands companion diagnostics portfolio with the launch of AI-powered Continuous Glucose Monitor DiasensTM and GlucoLiveTM DiasensTM and GlucoLiveTM are designed for diabetic, Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD), and post- transplant patients who require continuous glycaemic surveillance and integrated remote care The next-gen CGM is paired with AI-powered analytics and a dedicated clinician remote- monitoring dashboard which is integrated with GoodFlipTM app designed by TatvaCareTM Ahmedabad, 12 March, 2026 Zydus Lifesciences Limited (“Zydus”), an innovation-led global life sciences company, today announced its new offering companion diagnostics portfolio. The company will be launching DiasensTM and GlucoLiveTM, next-generation Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) devices that combine artificial intelligence- powered insights, and integrated remote care capabilities. The CGM system is designed to monitor glucose with an integrated AI layer to provide analytics and enable a closed-loop care ecosystem connecting patients, caregivers, and clinicians in real time. Zydus has partnered with Digicare Health Solutions Private Limited (TatvaCareTM), a healthcare technology company to help the patients gain access to its propri

summaries.Fund Raise / QIP / NCD:
## Fund Raise / QIP / NCD

---

- **Manorama Industries Limited (MANORAMA)** — The board met on March 12, 2026 to decide on raising funds through a QIP (selling new shares to big institutional investors), though no specific amount or price has been disclosed yet.
📊 **Impact:** Positive (cautiously) — When a company raises fresh money, it usually means they want to grow or expand; but it also means existing shareholders' ownership gets slightly diluted, so the stock may dip a little before recovering.
👀 **Watch:** The actual QIP price announced — if it's at a steep discount to the current market price, that's a red flag for existing shareholders.

---

- **Artificial Electronics Intelligent Material Ltd (526443)** — The company had applied on June 19, 2025 for in-principle approval to issue fully paid-up equity shares through conversion of warrants on a preferential basis, with no specific share count or price disclosed in the announcement.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — This is a procedural update about an old application; no new money is confirmed yet, so there is no immediate change in the company's financial situation.
👀 **Watch:** Whether the exchange grants final approval and at what price the warrants will be converted into shares.

---

- **Star Housing Finance Ltd (539017)** — The company has issued a notice regarding the exercise of a "Put Option" on its Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs), meaning debenture holders may have the right to demand early repayment of their money.
📊 **Impact:** Negative (mild) — If many debenture holders exercise this option and ask for their money back early, the company will need to arrange that cash quickly, which could strain its finances temporarily.
👀 **Watch:** Whether the company has enough liquid cash to honour the put option without needing to borrow again at higher rates.

---

- **Purple Finance Ltd (544191)** — The company has disclosed that it has made an interest payment to its NCD (Non-Convertible Debenture) holders, though the exact amount paid is not specified in the announcement.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — Paying interest on time is simply what a company is supposed to do; it shows the company isn't defaulting, but it's not exciting news that would move the stock.
👀 **Watch:** Whether future interest payments are also made on schedule, as any delay would be a serious warning sign for this finance company.

---

- **Nicco Uco Alliance Credit Ltd (523209)** — The board has approved a preferential issue of up to 14,58,333 equity shares, with the board meeting notice filed under Regulation 29(2) of SEBI's listing rules.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral to mildly negative — The company is planning to issue new shares to select investors, which means existing shareholders will own a slightly smaller piece of the same pie unless the money raised is used very productively.
👀 **Watch:** The price at which these 14.58 lakh shares are allotted — a price significantly below the market rate would hurt existing shareholders most.

---

- **Diligent Media Corporation Limited (DNAMEDIA)** — Despite being filed under DNAMEDIA's name, the document actually discloses the resignation of the Chief Executive Officer of the company, not a fund-raise by Sadhana Nitrochem.
📊 **Impact:** Negative — Losing a CEO creates uncertainty about who
summaries.Acquisitions & M&A:
Here is the pre-market M&A briefing:

---

- **Western Ministil Ltd (504998)** — The company's board has approved acquisition of 100% equity shares of Micron Calcite Private Limited, with a corrigendum filed to correct the earlier board meeting outcome dated 12th March 2026.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral-to-Positive — The company is buying a smaller private firm outright, which could add new business capacity, but details like the price paid and what Micron Calcite actually does are not yet disclosed, so it's too early to judge the full benefit.
👀 **Watch:** Whether the company discloses the acquisition price and Micron Calcite's revenue contribution in the follow-up filing.

---

- **Gravita India Limited (GRAVITA, Minerals & Mining)** — Gravita India has signed definitive agreements to acquire Rashtriya Metal Industries Limited, with details filed under Regulation 30 of SEBI LODR.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — Gravita is expanding its recycling and metals business by buying another metals company, which could mean more customers, more capacity, and higher revenues going forward.
👀 **Watch:** The acquisition price and whether Rashtriya Metal Industries brings any debt onto Gravita's books.

---

- **JK Lakshmi Cement Limited (JKLAKSHMI, Cement & Cement Products)** — The company filed an intimation under Regulation 30 of SEBI LODR; however, specific details of the acquisition or transaction have not been disclosed in the available document extract.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — No concrete numbers or deal details are available yet, so there is nothing actionable to assess at this stage.
👀 **Watch:** The full disclosure filing from JK Lakshmi that reveals what exactly this transaction involves.

---

- **Power Grid Corporation of India Limited (POWERGRID, Power)** — POWERGRID has acquired NES Pune East New Transmission Limited through the Tariff-Based Competitive Bidding (TBCB) route; no financial value has been disclosed in the headline.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — Power Grid is adding another transmission project to its portfolio, which means steady, government-backed income flowing in for many years, like adding a new toll road to a highway company's list.
👀 **Watch:** The project cost and the timeline by which NES Pune East New Transmission starts contributing revenue to POWERGRID.

---

- **Oxford Industries Ltd (514414)** — A Public Announcement has been submitted to BSE by Navigant Corporate Advisors under Regulations 3(1), 4, and 15(1) of the SEBI Takeover Code, signalling an open offer for acquisition of shares from public shareholders.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — When an open offer is triggered, the acquirer must buy shares from public shareholders at a declared price, which is usually higher than the current market price — this is good news for existing shareholders.
👀 **Watch:** The open offer price per share once the full public announcement document is published.

---

- **AGI Infra Limited (AGIIL, Realty)** — The company filed a Regulation 30 disclosure; no specific acquisition details, deal size, or target company name is available in the document extract provided.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — Routine compliance filing with no numbers or deal specifics disclosed; no immediate impact on the stock can
summaries.Regulatory & BSE Queries:
• **Accel Ltd (517494)** — The company received a copy of an NCLT Chennai order dated March 10, 2026, the full details of which are not disclosed in the headline.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — A court order from NCLT can be serious or routine; without knowing what the order says, we cannot call it good or bad news yet.
👀 **Watch:** What the NCLT Chennai order actually directs the company to do — approval, rejection, or penalty will determine the real impact.

---

• **Fedders Holding Ltd (511628)** — The company received an Adjudication Order from SEBI dated March 11, 2026, though the penalty amount is not disclosed in the announcement.
📊 **Impact:** Negative — When SEBI passes an adjudication order against a company, it usually means a fine or punishment for breaking market rules, which is a red flag for investors.
👀 **Watch:** The exact penalty amount and whether the company plans to appeal the SEBI order at the Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT).

---

• **Axis Bank Ltd (AXISBANK, Banks)** — Axis Bank informed exchanges of an analyst/institutional investor meet held on March 12, 2026, and separately scheduled another analyst meet for March 17, 2026. *(Note: The Biocon FDA approval headline appears misattributed to Axis Bank in the filing.)*
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — Routine compliance filing; meeting analysts and investors is standard practice and does not by itself move the stock price.
👀 **Watch:** Any new management guidance on loan growth, margins, or asset quality that may emerge from the presentation shared at the March 17 meet.

---

• **Carraro India Limited (CARRARO, Auto Components)** — Carraro India intimated exchanges of a scheduled meeting with analysts and investors, under Regulation 30 of SEBI's listing rules. *(Note: The Raymond Realty/Income Tax headline in the subject line appears misattributed.)*
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — Routine compliance filing; simply telling the market that a management-investor conversation will happen, with no new financial data shared yet.
👀 **Watch:** What management says about order pipelines and margins from the auto components business when the meeting details are published.

---

• **JNK India Limited (JNKINDIA, Industrial Manufacturing)** — JNK India filed an intimation with BSE and NSE under Regulation 30; the full content of the filing is not visible in the extract. *(Note: The Tata Steel SCN headline appears misattributed.)*
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — Routine compliance filing based on available information; no financial impact can be determined from the truncated document.
👀 **Watch:** The complete filing content to confirm whether this relates to a new order win, which would be positive for this industrial equipment maker.

---

• **Metropolis Healthcare Limited (METROPOLIS, Healthcare Services)** — Metropolis scheduled an analyst/institutional investor meeting with DAM Capital for March 23, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. IST. *(Note: The Bhageria Industries headline is misattributed.)*
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — Routine investor outreach; the company is simply opening a conversation with big investors, which is normal practice and has no direct effect on the stock.
👀 **Watch:** Any updated guidance on diagnostic test volumes,
summaries.Mergers & Schemes:
• **Ambuja Cements Ltd. (AMBUJACEM)**
The scheme of arrangement to merge Sanghi Industries into Ambuja Cements has been declared effective, with a record date being fixed for share entitlement.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — Ambuja is officially absorbing a rival cement maker, which means it gets bigger, gains more capacity, and could sell more cement across India, which is generally good for its business.
👀 **Watch:** The exact record date announced — shareholders of Sanghi on that date will receive Ambuja shares, so the swap ratio terms will determine whether existing Ambuja shareholders gain or get diluted.

---

• **Sanghi Industries Ltd. (SANGHIIND)**
The scheme of arrangement merging Sanghi Industries with Ambuja Cements has been declared effective, with a record date being set for the share swap.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — Sanghi shareholders will receive Ambuja Cements shares in exchange, meaning they are being absorbed into a much larger, stronger company backed by the Adani Group.
👀 **Watch:** Whether Sanghi shares are suspended from trading once the record date passes, as the stock will effectively cease to exist as an independent listed company after the merger completes.

---

• **Valor Estate Ltd. (DBREALTY)**
The scheme of amalgamation involving Valor Estate has been assigned an effective date, formally triggering the merger process.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — This is a procedural milestone confirming the merger is legally underway, but no financial details like share swap ratios or deal value are disclosed in this announcement, so there is nothing concrete to react to yet.
👀 **Watch:** The official share allotment or cancellation notice that follows, which will reveal whether existing DBREALTY shareholders gain, lose, or break even from the amalgamation terms.

---

• **Concord Enviro Systems Ltd. (CEWATER)**
The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) has passed an order in relation to the scheme of arrangement involving Concord Enviro Systems, though no specific financial terms or deal size are disclosed in this announcement.
